title: zea mexicana subsp. parviglumis
intro: Zea mexicana subsp. parviglumis, also known as Tohono O'odham sweet corn, is a subspecies of wild corn native to the Sonoran Desert in North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to its high content of anthocyanins and other polyphenolic compounds. The health benefits of Zea mexicana subsp. parviglumis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against certain diseases, such as cancer and diabetes. Zea mexicana subsp. parviglumis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of menopausal hot flashes.
